source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Ensure to Implement Access Control and Authentication

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Ensure to Implement Access Control and Authentication
input_row.description: Configure authentication mechanisms for your DocumentDB instances, such as using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users or database users. Define appropriate user roles and permissions to control access to the DocumentDB instances and databases.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign into the AWS Management Console • Sign into the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon DocumentDB Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/docdb/. 3. Select the DocumentDB Cluster • Choose the Amazon DocumentDB cluster for which you want to implement access control and authentication. • Click on the cluster name to access its details page. • In the cluster details page, navigate to the "Configuration" section. 4. Enable Authentication • Under the Network & Security section. • Click on the Edit button or Modify option to configure the authentication settings. • Enable the option for authentication by choosing the appropriate setting. • DocumentDB supports authentication through username and password or through AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) roles. 5. Configure Database Users • In the cluster details page, navigate to the Users or Database users section. • Click the Add user button to create a new database user.  • Enter the username and password for the database user. • Assign appropriate permissions to the user, such as read-only or read-write access to specific databases or collections. 6. Save the Configuration • Click on the Save button to apply the authentication and access control configuration. • DocumentDB will enforce authentication for connections to the cluster. 7. Test Authentication • Validate that your client applications or tools can connect to the DocumentDB cluster using the configured authentication credentials. • Ensure that the authentication process is successfully completed. 8. Monitor and Manage Access Control • Regularly monitor and manage the access control configuration for your DocumentDB cluster. • Review and update the permissions assigned to database users as needed. • Remove any unnecessary or unused database users to minimize security risks. 9. Consider IAM Authentication (Optional) • If desired, you can also configure IAM authentication for your DocumentDB cluster. • Follow the AWS documentation to set up IAM authentication for DocumentDB, if applicable.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

This control requires configuring access control and authentication for Amazon DocumentDB, which can be effectively verified through AWS APIs. DocumentDB provides AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) integration and allows setting up database users with specific roles and permissions. These configurations can be checked using AWS APIs such as boto3 or AWS CLI, making the audit process automatable.

✓ AWS APIs can query the access control and authentication settings of DocumentDB clusters.
✓ DocumentDB is a standard AWS service with IAM roles and authentication integration, which is automatable.
✓ boto3 SDK and AWS CLI have commands to retrieve and check IAM roles and user configurations for DocumentDB.
✗ Does not require SSH/RDP access for verification — configurations are managed through AWS services.
✗ The audit field, while not including API/CLI commands directly, describes tasks performed in the AWS Management Console, which corresponds to API actions.

Given this, the control is suitable for automated verification.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_docdb_cluster_access_control_and_authentication_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **AWS API Calls / boto3 SDK methods:**
  - Use `boto3` to access DocumentDB-specific operations:
    - Call `boto3.client('docdb').describe_db_clusters()` to retrieve cluster configurations and ensure authentication is enabled.
    - Use `boto3.client('iam').list_roles()` to verify IAM roles if IAM authentication is used.
    - Use `boto3.client('docdb').describe_db_cluster_parameter_groups()` to review database user parameter groups (if applicable).

- **AWS CLI Commands (if applicable):**
  - `aws docdb describe-db-clusters` to list clusters and their configurations.
  - `aws iam list-roles` to check IAM roles related to DocumentDB clusters.

- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Enumerate DocumentDB clusters across all regions available to the organization to ensure access control and authentication mechanisms are implemented.

- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Check that each DocumentDB cluster has authentication enabled via IAM or via database user configurations.
  - Validate that user roles and permissions are appropriately set and align with the principle of least privilege.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** Authentication is enabled for all DocumentDB clusters, with roles and permissions correctly set, either via IAM or assigned database users.
  - **Fail:** Any DocumentDB cluster found without proper authentication mechanisms or misconfigured access controls.

- **Example:**
  - Utilize `boto3` to confirm that each DocumentDB cluster's network and security settings have IAM roles integrated or proper database user credentials established.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The configurations for access control and authentication in Amazon DocumentDB can indeed be verified using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI, making this process suitable for automation. AWS provides comprehensive API support for querying DocumentDB configurations and IAM roles, which aligns with the automated approach.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- The automation details are mostly accurate, but there are some areas for improvement:
  - The `boto3.client('docdb').describe_db_clusters()` method is correctly identified for retrieving cluster configurations. However, it should be noted that this method primarily provides information about the clusters themselves and not directly about IAM authentication settings.
  - The `boto3.client('iam').list_roles()` method is valid for listing IAM roles, but it doesn't directly verify the roles associated with DocumentDB clusters. Instead, checking the `AssociatedRoles` attribute from `describe_db_clusters()` would be more relevant.
  - The use of `boto3.client('docdb').describe_db_cluster_parameter_groups()` is not directly related to authentication settings. Instead, checking the `EngineMode` and `AssociatedRoles` attributes in the cluster description would be more pertinent.
  - The AWS CLI commands listed are valid, but similar to the boto3 methods, they need to focus more on the specific attributes related to authentication and access control.

- The program name `aws_docdb_cluster_access_control_and_authentication_verified` is somewhat descriptive but could be more specific to the exact checks being performed.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Missing Considerations:**
  - Include checks for the `AssociatedRoles` attribute in the `describe_db_clusters()` response to verify IAM role integration.
  - Consider using AWS Config rules to automatically assess compliance with IAM authentication settings for DocumentDB.
  - Ensure that the audit checks for both IAM authentication and database user authentication, as both are mentioned in the control description.

- **Alternative AWS API Methods:**
  - Consider using `boto3.client('docdb').describe_db_cluster_parameters()` to check specific parameter settings related to authentication if applicable.

- **Better Program Name Suggestion:**
  - `aws_docdb_authentication_and_access_control_check` – This name is more concise and clearly indicates the focus on authentication and access control.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- While the general approach is sound, the technical details require refinement to ensure all relevant aspects of the control are thoroughly checked. The confidence level is medium due to the need for more precise targeting of the API methods and attributes relevant to the control.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Adjust the automation details to focus on the specific attributes and methods that directly relate to authentication and access control settings in DocumentDB. Ensure that the checks cover both IAM and database user authentication as outlined in the control description. Consider refining the program name for clarity.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that access control and authentication settings for Amazon DocumentDB clusters can be verified via AWS APIs. Specifically, the `describe_db_clusters` API exposes the `AssociatedRoles` attribute for IAM integration and other cluster-level authentication settings. While some details about database users may require additional steps, the core compliance check—verifying IAM authentication and associated roles—is fully automatable using AWS SDKs and CLI. No manual console inspection or SSH/RDP is required.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_docdb_cluster_access_control_and_authentication

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: Amazon DocumentDB Clusters
- API/CLI Method: 
  - boto3: `client('docdb').describe_db_clusters()`
  - AWS CLI: `aws docdb describe-db-clusters`
- Properties to Check:
  - `AssociatedRoles` (should include relevant IAM roles for authentication)
  - `IAMDatabaseAuthenticationEnabled` (should be `True` if IAM authentication is required)
  - Any cluster-level parameters indicating authentication mechanisms
- Pass Criteria:
  - At least one IAM role is associated with the cluster (via `AssociatedRoles`)
  - `IAMDatabaseAuthenticationEnabled` is set to `True` (if IAM authentication is required by policy)
  - No clusters with open/public access or default/weak users without authentication
- Fail Criteria:
  - No IAM roles associated with the cluster
  - `IAMDatabaseAuthenticationEnabled` is `False` (if IAM authentication is required)
  - Presence of clusters with default/anonymous access or missing authentication configuration

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation, but the second AI clarified the exact API attributes to check. This final decision incorporates those specifics for technical accuracy.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
